# servicenow_dictionary (Resource)

`servicenow_dictionary` manages a column definition (sys_dictionary entry) for a table within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a column definition (sys_dictionary entry) for a table in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_db_table" "example" {
  label     = "Example Table"
  user_role = ""
}

resource "servicenow_dictionary" "example" {
  name          = servicenow_db_table.example.name
  element       = "u_example_field"
  column_label  = "Example Field"
  internal_type = "string"
  max_length    = 255
  mandatory     = false
  active        = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `element` (String) The internal name of the column (field) being defined.
- `name` (String) The internal name of the table this column belongs to.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Flag indicating if this dictionary entry is active.
- `choice` (Number) Choice type for the column. 0 = none, 1 = suggestion, 3 = dropdown.
- `column_label` (String) Human readable label for the column.
- `comments` (String) Free form comments describing this column.
- `default_value` (String) Default value applied to the column when a new record is created.
- `dependent` (String) Name of a column on which this column's choices depend.
- `dependent_on_field` (String) Name of the field this column depends on for dynamic display.
- `display` (Boolean) If true, this column is the table's display column.
- `dynamic_creation` (Boolean) If true, this column is dynamically created at runtime.
- `internal_type` (String) Internal data type of the column (e.g. 'string', 'integer', 'boolean', 'glide_date', 'glide_date_time', 'reference', 'choice').
- `mandatory` (Boolean) If true, the column must contain a value.
- `max_length` (Number) The maximum length permitted for values stored in the column.
- `read_only` (Boolean) If true, the column cannot be edited via the standard UI.
- `reference` (String) Target table name when 'internal_type' is 'reference'.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))
- `unique` (Boolean) If true, values stored in the column must be unique across rows.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String)
- `delete` (String)
- `read` (String)
- `update` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_dictionary.example <sys_id>
```
